name: Chicken in Silky Almond Sauce
keywords: indian main dish chicken spicy ethnic
recipeInstructions:
1. Pat the chicken dry.
2. Heat a smaller amount of oil in a heavy large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
3. Add the chicken in batches and cook on all sides just until no longer pink (do not brown). Remove using a slotted spoon and set aside.
4. Heat the remaining oil in the skillet. Add the sliced onions and fry until wilted and pale brown, stirring constantly to color evenly, about 12 minutes.
5. Stir in the almonds, coriander, ginger, cardamom, ground red pepper, cumin, and fennel and cook for 3 to 5 more minutes.
6. Remove the mixture from the heat.
7. Transfer half of the mixture to a processor or blender. Puree with 1/2 of the yogurt and 1/2 of the water. Repeat with the rest of the mixture, yogurt, and water.
8. Pour the sauce back into the skillet. Add the chicken to the skillet. Place over medium-high heat and bring to a boil.
9. Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer until the chicken is tender and the sauce is thickened, about 45 minutes.
10. Remove from the heat. Let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes (the dish is best refrigerated and reheated). Rewarm over low heat. Transfer to a serving dish, garnish with cilantro, and serve immediately.
